manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Please help me with the following task. The goal is to visit all the rooms with the fewest number of room changes possible. In each room, you need to decide the direction to go in. Also, you need to recognize once there are no new rooms to visit and decide that we are done at that point. Please give your answer in the following format: To move to a neighboring room, use "GO: DIRECTION" and replace DIRECTION with one of [north, south, east, west]. To stop the exploration, answer with "DONE" instead. Omit any other text.
Here is an example:
You are in the Kitchen. Currently available directions: south, west. What is your next instruction?
GO: west
You have made a step and entered a Lobby. Currently available directions: east, north. What is your next instruction?
GO: north
...
You have made a step and entered a Bedroom. Currently available directions: south. What is your next instruction?
DONE
Let us start. You are in the Guest room. Currently available directions: north. What is your next instruction?

The user wants me to solve a pathfinding problem on a graph of rooms.
The goal is to visit all rooms with the fewest number of room changes possible.
However, "fewest number of room changes" usually implies finding a path that visits every node (or covers all nodes) efficiently. In graph theory, visiting all nodes is often called the "Graph Exploration" or "Search" problem. Since I don't have the map upfront, I must explore dynamically.
The prompt says: "In each room, you need to decide the direction to go in. Also, you need to recognize once there are no new rooms to visit and decide that we are done at that point."
This sounds like a Depth-First Search (DFS) strategy. I need to explore until I reach a dead end, then backtrack to the last junction with unexplored paths.
Actually, re-reading the constraint: "fewest number of room changes possible".
If I just do a standard DFS, the "room changes" (moves) count will be $2(E)$ minus something, or just the path length of the traversal.
Since I don't see the whole map, I can't calculate the *optimal* TSP-style path. I have to act as an agent exploring.
The prompt asks me to "recognize once there are no new rooms to visit".
This implies I need to keep track of visited rooms.
